entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Indigenous crop of vital importance for food security and survival in Mali

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: The country where the study on sorghum seed systems is conducted

entities.name: TRADITIONAL SEED SYSTEM
entities.description: The traditional system used by Malian farmers for managing sorghum seeds, conceptualized as a commons

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: COMMONS
entities.description: A resource management system where a resource is shared and managed by a community

entities.name: COLLABORATIVE NETWORKS
entities.description: Networks consisting of farmer groups, public breeding programs, and development organizations

entities.name: PUBLIC BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs aimed at creating and improving crop varieties

entities.name: DECENTRALIZED CREATION AND DISSEMINATION
entities.description: The process of creating and spreading crop varieties through local networks rather than a central authority

entities.name: VARIETAL DIVERSITY
entities.description: The range of different varieties of a crop available within a system

entities.name: VARIETAL PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The measurable characteristics and productivity of specific crop varieties

entities.name: SINGLE-CASE STUDY
entities.description: A research approach focusing on a single instance or case to provide detailed analysis

entities.name: FOUNDATION SEED
entities.description: High-quality seed produced by breeding programs to be used for further multiplication

entities.name: COMMONING
entities.description: The social process of managing a resource as a commons

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain a process or system over time without depleting resources

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of a system to recover quickly from difficulties
